contents.value:
```ini
[Unit]
Description=My Web Application
After=network.target postgresql.service
Requires=postgresql.service
```
**[Unit]** section defines metadata and dependencies. `After` controls startup order. `Requires` enforces hard dependencies.

```ini
[Service]
Type=simple
User=www-data
ExecStart=/opt/app/server
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5
```
**[Service]** section defines how to run the service. Type, user, command, restart policy, and environment are set here.

```ini
Type=simple    # default, main process stays foreground
Type=forking   # traditional daemon (forks to background)
Type=oneshot   # run once and exit
Type=notify    # signals readiness via sd_notify()
```
**Type** defines the process start-up type. Most modern services use `simple`.

```ini
Restart=on-failure   # restart on non-zero exit, signal, timeout
Restart=always       # restart no matter what
Restart=no           # never restart (default)
RestartSec=5         # wait 5 seconds before restart
```
**Restart** controls automatic restart behavior. Use `on-failure` for production services.

```ini
[Timer]
OnCalendar=*-*-* 02:00:00   # daily at 2 AM
OnCalendar=Mon *-*-* 09:00  # Mondays at 9 AM
OnCalendar=hourly           # every hour
Persistent=true             # catch up missed runs
```
**Timer** units are the modern systemd alternative to cron. More flexible and integrated with logging.

```ini
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target  # start on boot (non-GUI)
WantedBy=graphical.target   # start on boot (GUI)
```
**[Install]** defines how `systemctl enable` sets up the unit. `WantedBy` creates a dependency symlink.

```ini
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=true
NoNewPrivileges=true
PrivateTmp=true
ReadOnlyPaths=/
ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/myapp
```
**Security hardening** restricts what the service can access. Use `systemd-analyze security myservice` to audit.
